purpose: Define the source-to-final-enrichment data flow and the execution contract for the final KM stack.

execution_flow.name: Source ingestion
execution_flow.input: Raw source document JSON record(s), including title, source metadata, source_url/final_url, body text, extracted HTML/text when available.
execution_flow.output: Raw source record with immutable trace IDs.

execution_flow.name: Evidence packet normalization
process: Normalize whitespace, preserve original language text, retain official title, jurisdiction/source metadata, dates, source_url/final_url, recitals/scope/purpose, definitions, operative provisions.
Create short evidence windows for title, scope, definitions, operative duties, sanctions, dates, and addressees.
Do not add benchmark labels, filenames, row answers, URL slugs as classification evidence.

execution_flow.name: Non-tag extraction
execution_flow.output: Date_LastChange, Topic1, Topic2, Topic3, ShortTitle, OriginalTitle, ShortDescription, TypeofRegulation, Source, Amendedordevelopedbyifany, Dateofadoption, EntryIntoForceDate, Sanctions

execution_flow.name: SpecificTopic family routing
execution_flow.output: Top 2 SpecificTopic family KM IDs by default, with a 3rd/4th family only when a separate central legal pillar is explicit in title, scope, definitions, or operative provisions.
process: High-precision default: route one narrow source to 1-2 families; do not fan out to 3-4 families unless the source has independent central legal pillars.

Governance/reporting is not a fallback route; run it only for central business-conduct, reporting/disclosure, due-diligence, anti-corruption, whistleblowing, lobbying, payment-practices, or compliance-architecture regimes.
Do not route Governance for ordinary permits, penalties, competent authorities, transposition, monitoring, implementation reports, or generic enforcement language.

execution_flow.name: SpecificTopic candidate generation
execution_flow.output: candidate_specific_topics with label, confidence, centrality, evidence_role, evidence_excerpt, why_tag, why_not_nearby_labels.
process: Each routed family KM normally emits at most 3 SpecificTopic candidates in high_precision_batch mode.
Candidates marked companion require a distinct central legal pillar; incidental/background/implementation-only candidates must be rejected or retained only in audit traces.

execution_flow.name: SpecificTopic reconciliation
execution_flow.output: Final SpecificTopic[] with rejected candidates and evidence.

execution_flow.name: ApplicableSectors classification and reconciliation
execution_flow.input: evidence_packet plus final SpecificTopic[] plus non-tag fields.
execution_flow.output: Final ApplicableSectors[] with direct-addressee/product/facility evidence roles and rejected sector candidates.

execution_flow.name: ESRS mapping
execution_flow.input: Final SpecificTopic[], evidence_packet, selected source mechanisms.
execution_flow.output: Final ESRS[] with mapping_trace and rejected ESRS candidates.

execution_flow.name: Schema assembly
execution_flow.output: One enriched final record matching the production schema.

execution_flow.name: Validation and repair
process: Validate all controlled values against canonical enums.
Require evidence_excerpt and tag_reason for retained labels.
Reject labels produced only from blocked evidence.
Enforce family candidate budget in high_precision_batch: normally <=3 candidates per family and <=2 routed families unless separate central pillars justify more.
execution_flow.output: Validated final enrichment record plus audit trace.

recommended_runtime_modes.high_precision_batch: Run router top 2 families by default; add a 3rd/4th only for separate central legal pillars. Run governance only when explicitly routed for business-conduct/reporting/due-diligence/compliance architecture. Each family normally emits <=3 SpecificTopic candidates, then strict reconciler.
recommended_runtime_modes.high_recall_audit: Run all nine SpecificTopic family KMs and keep rejected_candidates for evaluation.
recommended_runtime_modes.low_latency: Run router top 1-2 families. Do not run governance unless explicitly routed. Use strict family candidate budget and strict reconciler.

revision_note: v3.1 precision patch: reduces SpecificTopic candidate spray, tightens governance routing, adds per-family candidate budgets, strengthens label-specific reconciler gates, and makes sector/ESRS selection more downstream and evidence-role based.
